text: Priya, first round of holiday label concepts is in the shared folder. Three directions: warm minimal, botanical, and a hand-lettered serif. Curious which feels most like Harbor to you.
These are gorgeous. The hand-lettered one stopped me. Can we see it on the 8oz tin and the gift box?
On it. I'll render the tin wrap and the gift box and post both by Friday.
Quick note from fulfillment: gift boxes ship flat, so keep the lettering off the fold lines if you can.
Good catch, Noah. I'll pull the wordmark in from the seams and add a 4mm safe margin. Updated dielines coming.
Welcome aboard, Laura. This channel is for the winter campaign creative. Diane and I will review, Hannah's joining for styling direction.
Happy to be here. I'll start with the type system and a couple of header treatments so we lock the voice before layouts.
The earth-tone palette you sent reads beautifully against our product photography. Let's build the hero around it.
Agreed. For styling I'd keep props minimal so the lettering carries the frame. Laura, can we get a version with more negative space up top?
Yes. I'll raise the headline and let the photo breathe underneath. New comps tonight.
Final logo lockups, type specs, and the export pack are uploaded. SVG, PDF, and PNG at three sizes each.
Received, thank you. Usage rights confirmed on our end. This was a clean handoff.
Anyone have a Detroit letterpress shop they trust? Comparing quotes for a small invitation run.
Signal-Return in Eastern Market does lovely work and they're great with first-timers. Tell them Greg sent you.
I'm at capacity through the new year. If anyone has room for a small packaging project, I can make a warm intro.
I could take it on. Send the brief whenever and I'll let you know by end of week.
Posting a wedding monogram in progress. The ampersand is fighting me. Too ornate?
I'd thin the entry stroke and let the bowl carry the weight. Right now the two flourishes compete. Beautiful base though.
